name: Jamaaladeen Tacuma
abstract: Jamaaladeen Tacuma (born Rudy McDaniel; June 11, 1956) is an American jazz funk avant-garde bassist, composer and producer born in Hempstead, New York. He was a bandleader on the Gramavision label and worked with Ornette Coleman during the 1970s and 1980s, mostly in Coleman's Prime Time band.

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Newport Jazz Festival: Ornette Coleman and Prime Time/ Cecil Taylor Unit

about_quotes.speaker: Lisa Parrott
about_quotes.evidence: "Tales of Captain Black" which is another Ornette album, actually no, it’s a co-led album with Jamaaladeen Tacuma, Roland Shannon Jackson, Ornette, and Bern Nix on guitar?
about_quotes.doc_title: Lisa Parrott interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 22, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Paul Shaffer
about_quotes.evidence: he said you’ve got to get Jamaaladeen Tacuma on bass... These two guys and Tisziji and myself... that’s how this album was done.
about_quotes.doc_title: Paul Shaffer interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, August 24, 2023
